title: Von Ormy
name: Von Ormy
metaTitle: Von Ormy 1031 Exchange Services in San Antonio, TX
metaDescription: 1031 exchange coordination for Von Ormy investors evaluating I-35 corridor land banking, logistics-ready acreage, and annexation-exposed commercial property.

sections.html: Von Ormy is a small city southwest of San Antonio along I-35, incorporated in part to control annexation pressure from the larger city expanding down the corridor, and its commercial base today is dominated by undeveloped and lightly developed land rather than finished buildings. An investor considering Von Ormy should think in terms of land positioning and future use more than existing income streams.

sections.heading: Annexation History and Why It Still Matters
sections.html:
Von Ormy's incorporation was driven substantially by a desire to control its own zoning and tax base rather than be absorbed into San Antonio, and that history still shapes how land here gets entitled and developed. An investor should confirm current city boundaries and any pending annexation petitions before identifying a parcel, since jurisdiction over a specific piece of land has shifted before and can affect both zoning and tax treatment.

Property tax rates in Von Ormy also differ from the surrounding unincorporated county, so an investor comparing a Von Ormy parcel against a similarly priced tract just outside city limits should build both jurisdictions' current tax rates directly into the return model rather than assuming they are interchangeable.

sections.heading: Logistics-Ready Land Along the I-35 Corridor
sections.html:
Flat, undeveloped acreage along I-35 has drawn interest from logistics and distribution users looking for lower land costs than closer-in San Antonio submarkets, and several parcels here are being held speculatively in anticipation of that demand. An investor identifying raw land as replacement property should confirm utility availability and any existing development agreements, since the gap between owning land and having a leasable building can be longer than expected.

Rail access along portions of the corridor adds a further variable, since a parcel with an active or restorable spur commands different interest from logistics users than one without, and confirming which category a specific tract falls into should happen before it is placed on an identification list rather than after.

sections.heading: What a Von Ormy Search Currently Includes
sections.html:
Given the submarket's early stage of development, an identification list here typically covers:

  • undeveloped or lightly improved land along I-35
  • small logistics and distribution-ready acreage
  • rural residential and agricultural land held for future entitlement
  • early-stage retail pads near interchange access points

sections.heading: Entitlement, Utility, and Annexation Documentation
sections.html:
Before identifying any Von Ormy land parcel, an investor should pull current entitlement status, confirm water and sewer availability directly with the relevant utility provider rather than assuming San Antonio Water System coverage extends to the site, and check whether any annexation petition is pending that could change the applicable zoning code after purchase. A Phase I environmental review remains standard practice even on raw acreage with no prior structure.

A current survey and flood determination should also be pulled for any parcel near the drainage channels that cross this part of the county, since a portion of otherwise buildable acreage can sit within a mapped floodplain that limits where a future structure can actually be placed.

sections.heading: Timing an Exchange Around Land That Is Still Entitling
sections.html: Because much of what trades in Von Ormy is land rather than finished income-producing buildings, a lender's underwriting timeline can run longer than a standard purchase, so we recommend starting entitlement and utility confirmation well before the 45-day identification window closes. Investors coming out of a stabilized San Antonio building sometimes pair a Von Ormy land candidate with a finished, income-producing backup elsewhere in the metro under the three-property rule, since the two candidates carry very different risk profiles and should not be treated as interchangeable.

faqs.q: Is undeveloped land in Von Ormy a valid 1031 replacement property?
faqs.a: Yes, raw land held for investment qualifies as like-kind to other real property, but entitlement status, utility availability, and any pending annexation petitions should be confirmed before the property is identified.

faqs.q: Why does annexation history matter for a Von Ormy purchase?
faqs.a: The city's boundaries and jurisdiction over specific parcels have shifted before, which can affect zoning and tax treatment. Confirming current boundaries and any pending annexation petitions before identification avoids surprises after closing.

faqs.q: Does San Antonio Water System serve every Von Ormy parcel?
faqs.a: Not necessarily. Water and sewer availability should be confirmed directly with the relevant utility provider for the specific parcel rather than assumed, since coverage does not extend uniformly across the city.

faqs.q: Should I pair a Von Ormy land purchase with a different type of replacement property?
faqs.a: Often, yes. Land here carries a different risk profile than a finished, income-producing building, so investors coming out of a stabilized asset sometimes list a Von Ormy land candidate alongside a finished property elsewhere under the three-property rule.

faqs.q: Who holds my exchange funds if entitlement review takes longer than expected?
faqs.a: A qualified intermediary holds the funds for the full 180-day exchange period regardless of how long entitlement or utility confirmation takes, provided the closing occurs within that period.

faqs.q: Does rail access change how a Von Ormy land parcel should be identified?
faqs.a: It can. A parcel with an active or restorable rail spur draws different interest from logistics tenants than one without, so confirming which category applies to a specific tract should happen before identification rather than after.

faqs.q: Do Von Ormy property tax rates differ from the surrounding unincorporated county?
faqs.a: Yes, and an investor comparing a Von Ormy parcel against a similarly priced tract just outside city limits should confirm both jurisdictions' current rates directly rather than assuming they are interchangeable, since the difference affects long-term return.
